lesson.menu_desc: Compare subjects with bigger, easier, more interesting -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- School Subjects & Comparatives -- comparing with -er and more
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carolina Paludetto Rodrigues | Aula 14 -- School Subjects & Comparatives

listenings.text: This year I have many different subjects at school, and they are not all the same. For me, history is more interesting than geography, because I love stories about the past. Science is harder than history, but it is also more exciting, because we do real experiments in the lab. Math is the most difficult subject for me. The exams are longer and the problems are more complicated than in other classes. English is easier than math, and it is my favorite subject of all. My teacher says I am better at writing than at numbers, and I think she is right. Next month we have a big science project about the ocean, and I am more nervous about that than about my history exam. School is busier this year, but it is also more fun than last year.

Good morning, students. I have a few important announcements about this week. First, the science exam is now on Thursday, not on Wednesday, so you have one more day to study. The exam is shorter than last time, only twenty questions, but the questions are more difficult, so please review your notes. Second, the history project is bigger this year. You will work in pairs and your presentation must be longer than ten minutes. Remember, a clear project is better than a long project, so keep your ideas simple. Finally, the library is now open later, until six o'clock, which is more convenient for everyone. If you need a quiet place to study, the library is calmer than the cafeteria. Good luck with your exams, and have a great week.

extra_audio.text: Carolina is comparing her school subjects. First, she says that history is more interesting than geography. Then, she explains that science is harder than history. After that, she says that math is the most difficult subject of all. Finally, she decides that English is easier than math and it is her favorite subject.
